attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks with 25-35km remaining on sustained climbs (La Redoute, penultimate climbs), relying on time-trial power to sustain prolonged efforts rather than explosive accelerations. Also attacks inside final 200m on uphill sprint finishes to secure bonus seconds.
team_tactics: Frequently isolated on climbs with domestiques dropped early (3-7km from finish), forcing solo defense or attacks. Quick-Step provides early race control but lacks depth for sustained mountain pacing. Aggressively contests intermediate sprints and bonus seconds even when leading GC, obsessively banking time margins.
terrain_preference: Dominates time trials (45-48 second margins on 30km courses) and sustained gradient climbs (6-8%). Vulnerable on extreme gradients above 14%, high altitude efforts, and technical wet descents where crashes occur repeatedly. Performs best on cold/wet mountain finishes rather than heat.
vulnerability: Collapses catastrophically on multi-day high mountain stages (lost 27min Aubisque, 2min+ on sustained Alpine passes). Crashes frequently in technical/chaotic finishes. Shows mental fragility when conditions deviate from preparation (darkness complaints, earpiece removal). Poor sprint positioning skills cost winnable stages.
key_patterns: Attacks 25-35km out on sustained climbs with time-trial power, winning solo by 45+ seconds
Contests intermediate bonus seconds obsessively even when leading GC, banking marginal time gains
Isolated without teammates inside final 5km repeatedly, Quick-Step depth insufficient for mountain stages
Crashes in technical/wet conditions across multiple races, vulnerability in chaotic scenarios
Extraordinary recovery capacity: won KOM stage day after losing 27 minutes, rebounded from COVID quickly
Cracks on extreme gradients 14%+ and high altitude, losing 1:49-2:08 when exposed
form_trajectory: neutral: showed 2023 Vuelta extremes (catastrophic Aubisque collapse then immediate KOM dominance) but Lombardia defeat to Yates suggests persistent Alpine weakness. Time trial dominance remains consistent anchor.
